zh.All Saints Church, Sawtry, Huntingdon, E: 英國英格蘭亨廷格多史爾薩福崔
zh.lifeSummary: 當William Le Moyne of Powderich II於大約1320年出生在英國戴文波瑟利其時，他的父親William Le Moyne of Maddington47歲，母親Alice Trenchard37歲。他於1339年與Lady Margery Merton在英國英格蘭戴文結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他埋葬在英國英格蘭亨廷格多史爾薩福崔。

zh-hans.All Saints Church, Sawtry, Huntingdon, E: 英国英格兰亨廷格多史爾薩福崔
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Le Moyne of Powderich II于大约1320年出生在英国戴文波瑟利其时，他的父亲William Le Moyne of Maddington47岁，母亲Alice Trenchard37岁。他于1339年与Lady Margery Merton在英国英格兰戴文结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他埋葬在英国英格兰亨廷格多史爾薩福崔。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Moyne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 Norman, English: nickname from Old French moine, Anglo-Norman French muine, moingne ‘monk’. Some of the following early bearers without the definite article could alternatively belong with (2).
2  English:  see  Moon   (1).

variants.name: Moin
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Moyne, a nickname from Old French moine, Anglo-Norman French muine, moingne ‘monk’.
Muslim (mainly the Indian subcontinent): unexplained.

variants.name: Moon
meanings: Irish: variant of   Mohan  .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Moyon in La Manche. Compare   Mounce  .
Americanized form of German   Mohn   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Moynan
meanings: Irish: perhaps a diminutive of   Moyne   or a reduced form of   Moynihan  .

variants.name: Moine
meanings: French: from moine ‘monk’, probably applied as an occupational name for a servant at a monastery or an ironic nickname for someone of monkish behavior or appearance. Compare Lemoine .

variants.name: Moynes
meanings: English: variant of Moyne with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When William Le Moyne of Powderich II was born about 1320, in Potheridge, Devon, England, his father, William Le Moyne of Maddington, was 47 and his mother, Alice Trenchard, was 37. He married Lady Margery Merton in 1339, in Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He was buried in Sawtry, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Le Moyne of Powderich II est né environ 1320, à Potheridge, Devon, Angleterre, son père, William Le Moyne of Maddington, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Alice Trenchard, avait 37 ans. Il a épousé Lady Margery Merton en 1339, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Ilenterré à Sawtry, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Le Moyne of Powderich II um 1320 in Potheridge, Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Le Moyne of Maddington 47 und seine Mutter Alice Trenchard 37. Er hat 1339 in Devon, England, United Kingdom Lady Margery Merton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er wurde in Sawtry, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Le Moyne of Powderich II è nato il circa 1320 nel Potheridge, Devon, England, il suo padre, William Le Moyne of Maddington, aveva 47 anni e il suo madre, Alice Trenchard, aveva 37 anni. Lui sposato Lady Margery Merton nel 1339, nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Fu sepolto a Sawtry, Huntingdonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: William Le Moyne of Powderich IIが約1320年にイギリスデボンポスエリッジで生まれた時，父William Le Moyne of Maddingtonは47歳，母Alice Trenchardは37歳であった。1339年にイギリスイギリスデボンでLady Margery Mertonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼はイギリスイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアサウトリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Le Moyne of Powderich II(이)가 대략 1320년 영국 데폰 포더이지에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Le Moyne of Maddington(은)는 47세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Trenchard(은)는 37세였다. 그는 1339년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 Lady Margery Merton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드 헌틴그도실르 사w트리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Le Moyne of Powderich II nasceu aproximadamente 1320, em Potheridge, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Le Moyne of Maddington, tinha 47 anos e sua mãe, Alice Trenchard, tinha 37 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Margery Merton em 1339, em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele foi sepultado em Sawtry, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Le Moyne of Powderich II родился около 1320 г. в местности потиридж, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени William Le Moyne of Maddington было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Trenchard, 37. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Margery Merton в 1339 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он был похоронен в местности савтри, хантингдоншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Le Moyne of Powderich II nació aproximadamente 1320, en Potheridge, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, William Le Moyne of Maddington, tenía 47 años y su mamá, Alice Trenchard, tenía 37 años. Se casó con Lady Margery Merton en 1339, en Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Fue sepultado en Sawtry, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
